senses.definition: (of an action) done due to one's pride being hurt or not being treated well

examples.x: She storms out of the room in a fit of pique whenever someone disagrees with her.
We often witness him throwing things in a fit of pique when things don't go his way.
He tends to make impulsive decisions in a fit of pique, only to regret them later.
They argue with each other in a fit of pique, often saying hurtful things they don't mean.
She will likely react in a fit of pique if her ideas are not taken into consideration.
We fear he will walk away from the negotiation in a fit of pique if his demands are not met.

senses.definition: (of a situation) to keep getting worse in an uncontrollable manner

examples.x: The situation spins out of control as tensions escalate between the two parties.
The project is spiraling out of control due to mismanagement and lack of communication.
He spins out of control when faced with stressful situations, making impulsive decisions.
Their relationship spirals out of control as trust diminishes and conflicts intensify.
If conflicts are not resolved, their working relationship could spiral out of control.
He may spin out of control if he doesn't learn to manage his anger and emotions.

senses.definition: (of behavior) not in accordance with law and order and free from control

examples.x: The city's nightlife can get wild and woolly, with people engaging in reckless behavior and disregarding rules.
The protest turned wild and woolly as demonstrators clashed with the police, resulting in chaos and disorder.
The party became wild and woolly when guests started breaking things and causing disturbances.
Their behavior during the meeting was wild and woolly, with constant interruptions and disregard for the agenda.
The concert last night got wild and woolly, with the crowd becoming unruly and security struggling to maintain control.

word_list.word: on the spur of the moment

senses.definition: without any prior planning or thought

examples.x: She decides to go on a solo trip on the spur of the moment, booking a flight right away.
I usually choose my outfits on the spur of the moment, depending on my mood each day.
The couple decides to elope on the spur of the moment, disregarding their elaborate wedding plans.
He bought the vintage car on the spur of the moment, unable to resist its charm.
She regretted quitting her job on the spur of the moment without a backup plan.

word_list.word: in the heat of the moment

senses.definition: at a time when anger or excitement completely overwhelm one, preventing any rational thought to come to mind

examples.x: She often says hurtful things in the heat of the moment during arguments.
They tend to make impulsive decisions in the heat of the moment without considering the consequences.
I try to remain calm and composed, even in the heat of the moment.
We made some rash decisions in the heat of the moment, but we've learned from our mistakes.
They could salvage their friendship if they both learn to communicate calmly even in the heat of the moment.

senses.definition: to find it extremely difficult to control oneself, due to feeling really angry, excited, etc.

examples.x: They are beside themselves with anger after finding out about the betrayal.
I am beside myself with worry about my upcoming presentation.
He was beside himself with grief when he lost his beloved pet.
They were beside themselves with astonishment at the unexpected turn of events.

word_list.pronunciation: ɪnðə lˈæp ʌvðə ɡˈɑːdz ɔːɹ ɡˈɑːd

senses.definition: used to say that a situation or outcome is beyond human control or influence, and is left to fate, chance, or external forces to determine

examples.x: The outcome of the game is in the lap of the gods; we can only hope for the best.
They are anxiously waiting for the verdict, knowing that their fate is in the lap of the gods.
The team gave their best effort, but the victory was ultimately in the lap of the gods.
We prepared extensively, but the success of the project was ultimately in the lap of the gods.
If we give our all and work diligently, the outcome will not solely be in the lap of the gods.

word_list.pronunciation: lˈɛt wˈʌnz hˈɑːɹt ɹˈuːl wˈʌnz hˈɛd

senses.definition: to make a decision to do something solely based on one's emotions

examples.x: Despite the risks involved, she let her heart rule her head and followed her lifelong dream of becoming an artist.
He couldn't resist the temptation and let his heart rule his head, impulsively buying the expensive sports car.
In matters of love, it's often better to let your heart rule your head and listen to your feelings.
Letting your heart rule your head can be a double-edged sword, as it may bring joy but also lead to unexpected challenges.
She knew it was against her better judgment, but she let her heart rule her head and made the impulsive decision to quit her stable job and travel the world.

word_list.pronunciation: ɡɛt hæv ðə bˈɛst bˈɛɾɚ ʌv ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to be unable to conceal one's strong feeling or urge

examples.x: The sight of her favorite dessert got the best of her, and she couldn't resist taking a bite.
The anger he had been suppressing finally got the better of him, and he unleashed a torrent of heated words.
His uncontrollable laughter got the better of him, and he burst out laughing in the middle of the serious meeting.
The overwhelming joy she felt upon receiving the news got the best of her, and tears of happiness streamed down her face.
The excitement of being on stage got the best of her, and she couldn't help but break into a spontaneous dance.

senses.definition: used to refer to a situation in which a person or thing that is important or powerful is being led or controlled by a significantly less powerful or important person or thing

examples.x: In this company, it feels like the marketing department is the tail wagging the dog, as their demands and preferences dictate the overall business strategy.
The media coverage focused so much on a trivial controversy that it became a case of the tail wagging the dog, overshadowing the more significant issues at hand.
The demands of a small interest group have become the tail wagging the dog in the political landscape, distorting policies that should reflect the broader public interest.
After losing her job, he went stark raving mad, shouting and throwing things around the room.
The horror movie terrified her so much that she ended up running out of the theater stark raving bonkers.
She went raving mad when she won the lottery, jumping and screaming with joy.
The crowd at the concert was raving mad, cheering, dancing, and singing at the top of their lungs.
His raving mad rant about a conspiracy theory made everyone question his sanity.

senses.definition: to be unable to control oneself due to being under emotional or mental pressure

examples.x: My mother went to pieces after his death.

senses.definition: used to say that something is out of control or unpredictable

examples.x: His behavior started to veer off the rails after he lost his job.
The stock market was off the rails last week, with wild fluctuations and unpredictable changes.
The new software update was off the rails, causing glitches and crashes for users.

senses.definition: to unexpectedly switch from the planned path

examples.x: The project seemed promising, but it jumped the rails when unexpected challenges arose.
The company's expansion plans jumped the rails due to unforeseen economic downturns.
The political campaign jumped the tracks after a series of controversial statements by the candidate.
The peaceful protest jumped the tracks when a small group started engaging in disruptive behavior.
The team's well-organized project suddenly jumped the tracks when unforeseen challenges arose.
While discussing work strategies in the meeting, Sarah suddenly jumped the tracks and started talking about her weekend plans.
The professor, in the middle of a lecture on history, jumped the tracks and began discussing current technological advancements.

senses.definition: to allow oneself to behave freely and have fun

examples.x: After months of studying, she decided to cut loose and go on a spontaneous road trip with her friends.
The team celebrated their victory by cutting loose and dancing wildly in the locker room.
They decided to cut loose at the concert, singing along to their favorite songs and dancing with abandon.
After finishing the project, the group decided to cut loose and have a fun-filled weekend getaway.

senses.definition: an unexpected or unusual occurrence or event that alters the course of events

examples.x: By a quirk of fate, they both ended up at the same cafe on the same day and rekindled their friendship.
It was a quirk of fate that they missed their flight, but it led them to discover a hidden gem of a restaurant in the airport.
Through a quirk of fate, he stumbled upon the job opportunity of a lifetime while browsing the internet.
It was a quirk of fate that brought them together at that exact moment, and they knew it was meant to be.
He never expected to win the lottery—it was just a quirk of fate that he happened to buy a ticket that day.
